prompt: How do I create a commitment in this app?

score.finalText:
Go to the project, then open **Commitments** from the sidebar. That’s the app area for both subcontracts and purchase orders. Source: App Help article **“Commitments (Subcontracts and Purchase Orders)”** at `/docs/commitments`.

What to do:
1. Open the correct **project**
2. Click **Commitments**
3. Choose the commitment type:
   - **Subcontract** = trade labor + materials
   - **Purchase Order** = materials, equipment, or services
4. Create the new record and fill in the core fields:
   - vendor/company
   - title
   - contract number if needed
   - scope/description
   - dates
   - retainage if applicable
5. Save it, then build the **schedule of values (SOV)** if the workflow requires billing against it later
6. From there, manage:
   - commitment change orders
   - invoices/pay apps
   - status updates

The important distinction:
- Use a **subcontract** when you’re awarding work to a trade partner.
- Use a **PO** when you’re buying materials, equipment, or a simpler vendor service.

If you want, I can do more than explain it — I can draft the commitment for you right now. Send me:
- project name
- subcontract or PO
- vendor name
- title
- scope
- start date
- estimated completion date
- retainage %

And I’ll build the preview before anything gets written.

data.message: Reading conversation memory and project context

results.title: Commitments (Subcontracts and Purchase Orders)
results.description: Manage subcontracts, purchase orders, schedule of values, and commitment change orders.
results.excerpt: Commitments Commitments are the subcontracts and purchase orders issued for the project. Each commitment has a vendor, a schedule of values (SOV), and a workflow for change orders and invoices. Commitment Types - **Subcontract** — a contract with a trade subcontractor for labor and materials. - **Purchase Order (PO)** — a one-time or recurring order for materials, equipment, or services. Open Commitments 1. Select th

results.title: Submit an Invoice (Subcontractor)
results.description: Submit a pay application against your schedule of values.
results.excerpt: Submit an Invoice Subcontractors submit pay applications against the schedule of values (SOV) on their commitment. Each invoice bills a portion of each SOV line based on percent complete or amount earned. Open the Submission Form 1. Select the project. 2. Open **My Work**. 3. From the SOV section, select **Submit Invoice**, or 4. Open **Invoicing** > **New Subcontractor Invoice**. Fill in the Invoice 1. Select the **

results.title: Create or Invite a User
results.description: Add an app user from User Management and explain how the invited person finishes login setup.

actionCapabilities.label: Invite user
actionCapabilities.description: Prepare and send an invitation for a new Alleato OS user from User Management.
actionCapabilities.unavailableReason: The assistant can explain the invitation flow, but the invite-user write tool is not implemented yet.

results.excerpt: Create or Invite a User New users are added from User Management. In the current workflow, an administrator creates the user record, chooses the user's access, and sends an invitation email. The invited person accepts the invitation and creates their own password. The user's email address is their username. There is no separate username to give them. Steps 1. Open User Management. 2. Stay on the **App Users** tab. 3.

results.title: Ask the AI About Finances
results.description: Use the CFO advisor to analyze budgets, margins, cash flow, change order exposure, and live ERP data from Acumatica.
results.excerpt: Ask the AI About Finances The CFO advisor has live access to every financial record in the platform — and to your Acumatica accounting system. It analyzes through five lenses: margin protection, cash position, exposure, change order lifecycle, and billing and collections. Every financial answer is grounded in live data. The CFO never estimates or guesses — it tells you what the data shows and flags what it cannot det

results.title: Invoicing
results.description: Manage owner pay applications and subcontractor invoices against the schedule of values.
results.excerpt: Invoicing The Invoicing tool handles two flows: owner pay applications (billing the owner) and subcontractor invoices (subs billing the project). Both bill against an approved schedule of values. Open Invoicing 1. Select the project. 2. Open **Invoicing** from the sidebar. 3. input.message: How do I create a commitment in this app?

output.rationale: The user is asking how to perform an action within the application ('create a commitment'), which is a product-usage question rather than a project-status or document-evidence request. The appropriate route is app_help with an app workflow explanation.

output.reason: Streaming model tools enabled on the configured AI SDK provider. Vercel AI Gateway is preferred when AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY is configured; direct OpenAI requires AI_PROVIDER_PATH=openai or no gateway key. Disable by setting AI_ASSISTANT_DISABLE_STREAMING_MODEL_TOOLS=true.

memories.content: The user prefers detailed, specific updates on project status, risks, and next steps for Vermillion Rise Warehouse.
User prefers high-leverage, action-oriented tasks prioritized daily for Vermillion Rise Warehouse project.
User prefers to have tasks and RFIs associated with specific project names, due dates, and assignees documented clearly.
